name: bEASTside Kitchen
tagline: Covid pop-up turned Hawaii Kai neighborhood gem — live music nightly, garlic shrimp that rivals the North Shore trucks.
mustOrder: loco moco — elevated but still local
parking: Lot on Kalanianaole Hwy; ample compared to town restaurants
talkStory: Born as a pandemic pop-up tent in Waimanalo in September 2021 by James Kiko and his grandmother Nette, bEASTside grew into a Hawaii Kai brick-and-mortar that became the east side's living room. Live music nightly (except Tue), bingo on 2nd and 4th Mondays, and a crowd that's 100% local families. The umami garlic shrimp rivals any North Shore truck. Happy hour until 6pm, kitchen open until midnight most nights — zero tourists, pure community.
